execution_details.stdout:
Setting system policy to DEFAULT
Note: System-wide crypto policies are applied on application start-up.
It is recommended to restart the system for the change of policies
to fully take place.

llm_verdict.message:
The system cryptography policy has been set to the default policy with:

```bash
update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT
```

The command completed successfully (exit code 0), satisfying the **configure_crypto_policy** OpenSCAP rule. No further configuration changes are needed. If you have long‑running services (e.g., `sshd`, `httpd`, etc.), consider restarting them so they pick up the new policy immediately.

reasoning_messages.reasoning: The script fails because `var_system_crypto_policy` is left empty, so `update-crypto-policies --set` receives no argument. The default policy is `DEFAULT` (or `FIPS` if required). The remediation should set the variable to `DEFAULT` and then execute `update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT`. This resolves the error and satisfies the rule.
